steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Label the side pair for the named angle first: opposite, adjacent, and hypotenuse. Then pick the matching shortcut, such as SOH/CAH/TOA, complementary trig values, or a special right-triangle ratio.

steps.title: Find the side length
steps.content: The equilateral triangle has perimeter 858 cm. Since all three sides are equal: $$\text{Side} = \frac{858}{3} = 286 \text{ cm}$$

steps.title: Apply the circumradius formula for an equilateral triangle
steps.content: When the three vertices lie on a circle, that circle is the circumcircle. For an equilateral triangle with side length $s$, the circumradius is found using the formula. Substituting $s = 286$: $$R = \frac{286\sqrt{3}}{3}$$

steps.title: Solve for w
steps.content: The problem states the radius equals $w\sqrt{3}$. Setting our expression equal to this form: $$\frac{286\sqrt{3}}{3} = w\sqrt{3}$$Dividing both sides by $\sqrt{3}$: $$w = \frac{286}{3}$$
